{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "UI / UX Designer",
  "description": "Utiva Nigeria&nbsp;-&nbsp;We are a technology education and talent accelerator platform helping African tech talent gain in-demand skills and connect with global opportunities.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: UI / UX Designer

Job Location:&nbsp;Remote
Employment Type: Contract

Role Overview


	We are seeking a UI/UX Designer to transform complex ideas into intuitive, scalable product experiences.
	You will collaborate with Product, Engineering, and Growth teams to design user-centric web and mobile interfaces from discovery to delivery.


Key Responsibilities


	Translate requirements into user flows, wireframes, and prototypes
	Conduct user research and iterate designs based on insights
	Design end-to-end experiences across multiple platforms
	Create clean, consistent UI aligned with brand guidelines
	Maintain design systems and ensure cross-platform consistency
	Collaborate with Product Managers and Engineers to deliver feasible solutions


Requirements


	2&ndash;5 years of experience in UI/UX or Product Design
	Strong portfolio demonstrating UX thinking and UI execution
	Proficiency in Figma (components, auto-layout, design systems)
	Solid understanding of usability, accessibility, and UX principles
	Ability to think in systems, not just screens


Nice to Have:


	Experience in SaaS, EdTech, or marketplace products
	Basic knowledge of front-end constraints (HTML/CSS).
	Startup environment experience


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and portfolio to:&nbsp;wuraola@utiva.co&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-13",

  },

UI / UX Designer Job at Utiva Nigeria




        
    

        
        Utiva Nigeria - We are a technology education and talent accelerator platform helping African tech talent gain in-demand skills and connect with global opportunities.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: UI / UX Designer

Job Location: Remote
Employment Type: Contract

Role Overview


	We are seeking a UI/UX Designer to transform complex ideas into intuitive, scalable product experiences.
	You will collaborate with Product, Engineering, and Growth teams to design user-centric web and mobile interfaces from discovery to delivery.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Translate requirements into user flows, wireframes, and prototypes
	Conduct user research and iterate designs based on insights
	Design end-to-end experiences across multiple platforms
	Create clean, consistent UI aligned with brand guidelines
	Maintain design systems and ensure cross-platform consistency
	Collaborate with Product Managers and Engineers to deliver feasible solutions


Requirements


	2–5 years of experience in UI/UX or Product Design
	Strong portfolio demonstrating UX thinking and UI execution
	Proficiency in Figma (components, auto-layout, design systems)
	Solid understanding of usability, accessibility, and UX principles
	Ability to think in systems, not just screens


Nice to Have:


	Experience in SaaS, EdTech, or marketplace products
	Basic knowledge of front-end constraints (HTML/CSS).
	Startup environment experience


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and portfolio to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Personal Assistant",
  "description": "Lington &amp; Bernie Consulting Limited was established on October 8, 1999 to provide efficient and qualitative consulting services to the oil and gas industry as well as banks and financial institutions in the areas of human resources placement and management, human resources consulting services, tax administration and payroll services as well as oil and gas fiscal and contractual terms advisory services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Personal Assistant

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	A personal assistant (PA) provides essential support by managing schedules, handling communication, and performing various administrative tasks tailored to the needs of their employer.


Key Responsibilities


	Act as the point of contact between the manager and internal/external clients
	Screen and direct phone calls and distribute correspondence
	Handle requests and queries appropriately
	Manage diary and schedule meetings and appointments
	Make travel arrangements
	Take dictation and minutes
	Source office supplies
	Produce reports, presentations and briefs
	Devise and maintain office filing system


Requirements and skills


	Proven work experience as a Personal Assistant
	Knowledge of office management systems and procedures
	MS Office and English proficiency
	Outstanding organisational and time management skills
	Up-to-date with latest office gadgets and applications
	Ability to multitask and prioritize daily workload
	Excellent verbal and written communications skills
	Discretion and confidentiality
	Good Business Sense
	Financial Understanding
	Real Estate Understanding
	Follow thorough on issues
	A real Handy man.


Salary
N100,000 - N200,000 per month

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV to:&nbsp;jobs@lingtonandbernie.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Personal Assistant Job at Lington & Bernie Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        Lington & Bernie Consulting Limited was established on October 8, 1999 to provide efficient and qualitative consulting services to the oil and gas industry as well as banks and financial institutions in the areas of human resources placement and management, human resources consulting services, tax administration and payroll services as well as oil and gas fiscal and contractual terms advisory services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Personal Assistant

Job Location: Lagos Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	A personal assistant (PA) provides essential support by managing schedules, handling communication, and performing various administrative tasks tailored to the needs of their employer.


Key Responsibilities


	Act as the point of contact between the manager and internal/external clients
	Screen and direct phone calls and distribute correspondence
	Handle requests and queries appropriately
	Manage diary and schedule meetings and appointments
	Make travel arrangements
	Take dictation and minutes
	Source office supplies
	Produce reports, presentations and briefs
	Devise and maintain office filing system


Requirements and skills


	Proven work experience as a Personal Assistant
	Knowledge of office management systems and procedures
	MS Office and English proficiency
	Outstanding organisational and time management skills
	Up-to-date with latest office gadgets and applications
	Ability to multitask and prioritize daily workload
	Excellent verbal and written communications skills
	Discretion and confidentiality
	Good Business Sense
	Financial Understanding
	Real Estate Understanding
	Follow thorough on issues
	A real Handy man.


Salary
N100,000 - N200,000 per month

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Junior Architect",
  "description": "ATO Architects Limited is a leading architectural and construction firm dedicated to delivering innovative design and construction solutions. With a rich history of shaping environments and creating iconic structures, we are committed to excellence in design, sustainability, and project delivery.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Junior Architect

Job Location:&nbsp;&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Assist in developing architectural designs, concepts, and presentations
	Prepare detailed drawings using software such as AutoCAD, Revit, and SketchUp
	Support in producing working drawings, construction details, and specifications
	Conduct site visits to monitor project progress and ensure design compliance
	Assist in liaising with clients, consultants, and contractors
	Ensure designs comply with Nigerian building codes, safety regulations, and environmental standards
	Participate in project meetings and provide technical support
	Assist in preparing project documentation, reports, and BOQs (Bills of Quantities)
	Review materials and finishes suitable for local conditions and budget
	Support in obtaining approvals from relevant regulatory bodies (e.g., planning permits)


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree (B.Sc. or B.Tech) in Architecture
	0&ndash;2 years of post-NYSC experience
	Completion of NYSC is mandatory
	Proficiency in AutoCAD, Revit, SketchUp, and Microsoft Office
	Basic knowledge of Nigerian building regulations and approval processes
	Strong design, analytical, and problem-solving skills
	Good communication and teamwork abilities
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines


Professional Membership (Added Advantage):


	Student/Graduate membership with the Nigerian Institute of Architects (NIA)
	Registration with Architects Registration Council of Nigeria (or in view)


Key Competencies:


	Creativity and attention to detail
	Technical drawing and visualization skills
	Time management and organization
	Knowledge of local construction materials and methods
	Adaptability and willingness to learn


Working Conditions:


	Office-based with frequent site visits
	May require travel within project locations
	Occasional extended working hours to meet deadlines


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their Applications to:&nbsp;Info@atoarchitects.com&nbsp;using &quot;Architect&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Junior Architect Job at ATO Architects Limited




        
    

        
        ATO Architects Limited is a leading architectural and construction firm dedicated to delivering innovative design and construction solutions. With a rich history of shaping environments and creating iconic structures, we are committed to excellence in design, sustainability, and project delivery.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Junior Architect

Job Location:  Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Assist in developing architectural designs, concepts, and presentations
	Prepare detailed drawings using software such as AutoCAD, Revit, and SketchUp
	Support in producing working drawings, construction details, and specifications
	Conduct site visits to monitor project progress and ensure design compliance
	Assist in liaising with clients, consultants, and contractors
	Ensure designs comply with Nigerian building codes, safety regulations, and environmental standards
	Participate in project meetings and provide technical support
	Assist in preparing project documentation, reports, and BOQs (Bills of Quantities)
	Review materials and finishes suitable for local conditions and budget
	Support in obtaining approvals from relevant regulatory bodies (e.g., planning permits)


Requirements & Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree (B.Sc. or B.Tech) in Architecture
	0–2 years of post-NYSC experience
	Completion of NYSC is mandatory
	Proficiency in AutoCAD, Revit, SketchUp, and Microsoft Office
	Basic knowledge of Nigerian building regulations and approval processes
	Strong design, analytical, and problem-solving skills
	Good communication and teamwork abilities
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines


Professional Membership (Added Advantage):


	Student/Graduate membership with the Nigerian Institute of Architects (NIA)
	Registration with Architects Registration Council of Nigeria (or in view)


Key Competencies:


	Creativity and attention to detail
	Technical drawing and visualization skills
	Time management and organization
	Knowledge of local construction materials and methods
	Adaptability and willingness to learn


Working Conditions:


	Office-based with frequent site visits
	May require travel within project locations
	Occasional extended working hours to meet deadlines


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their Applications to: [email protected] using "Architect" as the subject of the email.

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Front Desk / Office Manager",
  "description": "Pace Technical &amp; Engineering Services Limited (PATES) is an engineering firm committed to delivering high-quality, innovative, and timely solutions across the building services industry. Operating in line with internationally recognized ISO 9001 quality standards, PATES specializes in feasibility studies, conceptual and detailed engineering design, project management, procurement, and field services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk / Office Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Gbagada, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are looking for a professional and highly organized Front Desk / Office Manager to oversee front office operations in a fast-paced engineering firm.
	The ideal candidate will manage administrative tasks efficiently, coordinate office activities, and serve as the first point of contact for clients, vendors, and engineers.


Key Responsibilities


	Welcome clients, contractors, and visitors, ensuring a professional first impression
	Handle incoming calls, emails, and official correspondence
	Manage office schedules, meetings, and appointments for engineers and management
	Maintain proper documentation, project files, and records
	Coordinate office supplies, equipment, and maintenance needs
	Liaise with vendors, contractors, and service providers
	Support basic HR and administrative functions (staff records, onboarding logistics)
	Ensure smooth day-to-day office operations


Requirements


	Minimum of OND / HND / BSc in Business Administration or related field
	2&ndash;4 years of experience in a similar administrative/front desk role (engineering or technical firm is an advantage)
	Strong organizational and multitasking skills
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools
	Ability to handle confidential information with discretion
	Professional demeanor and attention to detail


What We&rsquo;re Looking For:


	Someone who can work independently and take initiative
	A detail-oriented and structured individual
	Ability to manage multiple priorities in a technical work environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;leanhrsolutions@yahoo.com&nbsp;using &quot;Front Desk / Office Manager Application&quot; using the Job Title&nbsp;and location as subject of the mail.",

Front Desk / Office Manager Job at Pace Technical & Engineering Services Limited




        
    

        
        Pace Technical & Engineering Services Limited (PATES) is an engineering firm committed to delivering high-quality, innovative, and timely solutions across the building services industry. Operating in line with internationally recognized ISO 9001 quality standards, PATES specializes in feasibility studies, conceptual and detailed engineering design, project management, procurement, and field services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk / Office Manager

Job Location: Gbagada, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a professional and highly organized Front Desk / Office Manager to oversee front office operations in a fast-paced engineering firm.
	The ideal candidate will manage administrative tasks efficiently, coordinate office activities, and serve as the first point of contact for clients, vendors, and engineers.


Key Responsibilities


	Welcome clients, contractors, and visitors, ensuring a professional first impression
	Handle incoming calls, emails, and official correspondence
	Manage office schedules, meetings, and appointments for engineers and management
	Maintain proper documentation, project files, and records
	Coordinate office supplies, equipment, and maintenance needs
	Liaise with vendors, contractors, and service providers
	Support basic HR and administrative functions (staff records, onboarding logistics)
	Ensure smooth day-to-day office operations


Requirements


	Minimum of OND / HND / BSc in Business Administration or related field
	2–4 years of experience in a similar administrative/front desk role (engineering or technical firm is an advantage)
	Strong organizational and multitasking skills
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools
	Ability to handle confidential information with discretion
	Professional demeanor and attention to detail


What We’re Looking For:


	Someone who can work independently and take initiative
	A detail-oriented and structured individual
	Ability to manage multiple priorities in a technical work environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Front Desk / Office Manager Application" using the Job Title and location as subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply
